Title: Engineering of nicotianamine synthesis enhances cadmium mobility in plants and results in higher seed cadmium concentrations

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: nicotianamine; cadmium in seeds; iron in seeds; zinc in seeds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: metal chelator; mineral nutrients; toxic metal
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nicotianamine chelates transition metals and supports metal homeostasis and biofortification; seed Fe and Zn are essential micronutrients for human nutrition; Cd accumulation poses food safety risk
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: whole seedlings and leaves for nicotianamine; seeds for Fe, Zn, and Cd
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: nicotianamine synthesis branch for intracellular metal chelation and long-distance metal mobility
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies chelator pool that increases mobility and accumulation of Fe, Zn, and also Cd toward seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases nicotianamine accumulation, enhances Cd tolerance in seedlings, and increases seed Fe, Zn, and Cd concentrations; expression in yeast increases Cd uptake/accumulation and causes Cd hypersensitivity
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for nicotianamine level; supported as an effective engineering lever but not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression in Arabidopsis and yeast; mutant complementation in nas1nas2nas4; nicotianamine quantification by HPLC; elemental profiling by ICP-OES; root growth tolerance assays; subcellular localization by confocal microscopy; short-term Cd uptake assay in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtNAS2 overexpression caused a 5-8-fold increase in nicotianamine levels in Arabidopsis. 2. mCherry-AtNAS2 complemented nicotianamine-deficiency phenotypes of nas1nas2nas4 and conferred elevated Cd tolerance. 3. Under environmentally relevant soil Cd, AtNAS2-overexpressing lines accumulated about 2-4-fold more seed Fe and Zn and significantly more seed Cd. 4. In S. pombe and S. cerevisiae, AtNAS2 expression increased Cd accumulation/uptake and caused Cd hypersensitivity, supporting intracellular Cd-NA complex formation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: NA; metal excess (Zn or Cd) increased nicotianamine accumulation in seedlings, but gene-expression pattern of AtNAS2 itself was not directly reported here
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cd exposure; Zn excess; Ni excess; environmentally relevant Cd-contaminated soil; yeast thiamine withdrawal to induce nmt1-driven expression
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: UBQ10 promoter in Arabidopsis (constitutive); nmt1 promoter in Schizosaccharomyces pombe (thiamine-repressible/inducible); GAL1 promoter in Saccharomyces cerevisiae (inducible)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Unintended increase in seed Cd accumulation creates food safety risk during Fe/Zn biofortification; strong NAS expression inhibited growth in yeast liquid culture
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful biofortification target to raise seed Fe and Zn and improve metal mobility, but deployment should be combined with low-Cd germplasm or Cd-uptake reduction traits and careful monitoring of phytoavailable soil Cd
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study infers AtNAS2 action through nicotianamine-mediated chelation rather than direct Cd transport. Cytosolic localization was experimentally shown. Seed Cd increase is a key unintended consequence for biofortification strategies.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: nicotianamine-linked cadmium tolerance; nicotianamine-linked zinc tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports detoxification/tolerance to excess Cd and Zn through nicotianamine-dependent metal buffering
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies chelator pool required for Cd and Zn detoxification/tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of NAS4 function caused Cd hypersensitivity, and triple mutants lacking NAS4 were Cd hypersensitive; NAS4 also contributed strongly to Zn tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key contributor for Cd detoxification in roots; not established as pathway rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: single and triple mutant root growth assays under Cd or Zn excess
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. nas4 single mutants were significantly more Cd sensitive than Col-0. 2. All triple mutants lacking NAS4 showed Cd hypersensitivity, whereas nas1nas2nas3 resembled wild type. 3. NAS4 also contributes to Zn tolerance, especially in combination with NAS2 loss.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: excess Cd and Zn during seedling growth on vertical plates
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes metal hypersensitivity; overproduction context may also elevate Cd mobility when nicotianamine is increased overall
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant for tuning nicotianamine-mediated metal tolerance, but increasing NA synthesis should consider Cd co-mobilization risk
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Results suggest distinct nicotianamine pools and a specific role of NAS4 in Cd detoxification.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: nicotianamine-linked zinc tolerance; nicotianamine-linked cadmium tolerance
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports Zn detoxification/tolerance and, when overexpressed, enhances NA-mediated metal mobility
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots/seedlings; seeds indirectly via overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies chelator pool required for Zn tolerance and for increased metal mobility when overexpressed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: NAS2 contributes to Zn detoxification together with NAS4, and AtNAS2 overexpression elevates NA, Fe, Zn, and Cd accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for nicotianamine level; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: triple mutant analyses; transgenic overexpression; metabolite quantification; elemental profiling; tolerance assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Triple mutants lacking NAS2 and NAS4 were clearly more Zn sensitive than Col-0. 2. AtNAS2 overexpression rescued nas1nas2nas4 defects and raised NA content. 3. Overexpression increased seed Fe and Zn but also Cd.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: excess Zn; excess Cd; Cd-contaminated soil
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: NA increase improves biofortification but can elevate seed Cd
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for Fe/Zn biofortification engineering, ideally stacked with low-Cd uptake alleles
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This entry captures endogenous NAS2 genetic evidence in Arabidopsis, distinct from the specific transgene-focused AtNAS2 entry.
